jokes.text: A snake in the cash register! Yeah, great prank, fellas. Great.
jokes.setup: Moe discovers a snake in his cash register as part of a prank
jokes.punchline: His utterly deadpan, sarcastic response treating it like a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization - his weary acceptance of absurd bar pranks is both believable and funny

jokes.text: Hey, Moe, you wanna smell my flower? Do I!
jokes.setup: Barney offers Moe a flower to smell
jokes.punchline: Moe's enthusiastic response, likely followed by getting squirted
jokes.explanation: Classic squirting flower gag - predictable but well-executed

jokes.text: I'm burning up here! Taking advantage of my alcohol-soaked clothes.
jokes.setup: Moe got squirted by the flower
jokes.punchline: His complaint reveals his clothes are so alcohol-soaked they're flammable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail - reveals Moe's drinking problem while being absurdly specific

jokes.text: Oh, it's funny and it makes you think.
jokes.setup: Barney commenting on the prank aftermath
jokes.punchline: Treating a simple prank like highbrow entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious commentary on lowbrow humor

jokes.text: Oh, there's sugar all over the bar now. That's not funny, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer does something with sugar as a prank
jokes.punchline: Moe's fed-up response shows Homer crossed the line from pranks to property damage
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's tendency to take things too far, setting up the ban

jokes.text: This bar is like a tavern to me.
jokes.setup: Homer pleading not to be banned
jokes.punchline: Saying the bar is 'like a tavern' - they're literally synonyms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - sincere emotional appeal using redundant language

jokes.text: Sorry, Homer. You should've thought of that before giving me the sugar-me-do.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining why he's banning Homer
jokes.punchline: Turning 'Love Me Do' into 'sugar-me-do' to describe the prank
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Moe's tendency toward creative insults

jokes.text: I'm taking your caricature down from Mount Lushmore
jokes.setup: Moe listing punishments for Homer
jokes.punchline: 'Mount Lushmore' - Mount Rushmore but for drunks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun that creates a vivid mental image while being perfect for a bar

jokes.text: 'It's Raining Men'? Yeah, not no more it ain't.
jokes.setup: Homer's favorite song being removed from jukebox
jokes.punchline: Homer's favorite song is 'It's Raining Men' and Moe's emphatic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character detail that subverts expectations about Homer's musical taste

jokes.text: Joke's on them. I'm still alive.
jokes.setup: Homer has been banned from the bar
jokes.punchline: His defiant response treats survival as somehow spiting them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating basic existence as a victory

jokes.text: Did you know the Chinese use the same word for crisis as they do for opportunity? Yes. 'Cris-a-tunity.'
jokes.setup: Lisa sharing a philosophical observation about Chinese language
jokes.punchline: Homer's mangled portmanteau 'cris-a-tunity'
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons - Homer confidently creating fake knowledge with a memorable made-up word

jokes.text: And I'm gonna get drunker than I've ever been in my entire life.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring he'll find a new bar
jokes.punchline: His ambitious goal to achieve record drunkenness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's approach to problems through excess

jokes.text: Wow, classy. Good evening, sir. Would you please leave without a fuss right now? Okay.
jokes.setup: Homer admires a classy establishment and is greeted politely
jokes.punchline: Immediate polite rejection and Homer's compliant acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - the politeness makes the rejection funnier

jokes.text: Sammy, you're too old to go on a date with two twins on the same night you're supposed to marry Diane without Rebecca knowing.
jokes.setup: Homer enters what appears to be Cheers
jokes.punchline: Convoluted soap opera-like plot summary mixing Cheers characters
jokes.explanation: Good meta-reference but the joke is more about recognition than comedy structure

jokes.text: I'll make you a bet. If this affects my major-league comeback, I'll sell the bar.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Cheers parody
jokes.punchline: Sam's delusional belief he can still make a baseball comeback
jokes.explanation: Accurate character parody but relies on outside knowledge

jokes.text: Just give me another beer, you brain-dead hick. I'll kill you. I'll kill all of you! Settle down, Normie.
jokes.setup: Norm being refused service
jokes.punchline: Extreme violent outburst treated casually by others
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Norm's rage and casual response from others

jokes.text: I know. This lesbian bar doesn't have a fire exit!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes something is bothering him about a place
jokes.punchline: His concern is safety code violation, not that he's in wrong type of bar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - subverts expectation about what Homer would find problematic

jokes.text: Enjoy your deathtrap, ladies.
jokes.setup: Homer leaving the lesbian bar
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact warning about fire safety
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Homer's genuine concern for strangers' safety

jokes.text: Homer? Who is Homer? My name is Guy Incognito.
jokes.setup: Homer being recognized at Moe's despite trying to hide
jokes.punchline: His terrible disguise name that's clearly fake
jokes.explanation: Perfect fake name that sounds both fancy and obviously made up

jokes.text: Oh, my God. This man is my exact double. That dog has a puffy tail!
jokes.setup: Guy Incognito (Homer's doppelganger) appears
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by the dog mid-revelation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag - creates a mystery then immediately abandons it for dog observation

jokes.text: Shut up, liver! My liver hurts.
jokes.setup: Homer contemplating having to quit drinking
jokes.punchline: Arguing with his own organ, then acknowledging its pain
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy concept showing Homer's relationship with his own body

jokes.text: But I am a pilot. Where's your uniform? I stowed it safely in the overhead compartment.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be a pilot to drink at pilot bar
jokes.punchline: His quick thinking with aviation terminology
jokes.explanation: Decent improvisation showing Homer can occasionally think on his feet

jokes.text: Who wants to fly to the Windy City? I'll go! Me! I'm your man! Conditions are a bit windy. Well, on second thought, maybe I'll sit down.
jokes.setup: Homer eagerly volunteers to pilot a plane
jokes.punchline: Immediately backs down when he learns about windy conditions
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's bravado deflating quickly

jokes.text: You're not just impersonating a pilot so you can drink here, are you? Yeah. That's exactly why I'm here. You flyboys. You crack me up.
jokes.setup: Pilot asks if Homer is fake
jokes.punchline: Homer admits it completely, pilot thinks he's joking
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Homer's honesty is so unexpected it's dismissed as pilot humor

jokes.text: As a change of pace, I'm gonna let you do most of the work. I think you're ready for it, Alan.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing his copilot
jokes.punchline: Treating the copilot doing work as a 'change of pace' and favor
jokes.explanation: Good workplace comedy - Homer's backwards view of delegation

jokes.text: We'll need that to live.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably breaks something important on the plane
jokes.punchline: Alan's matter-of-fact assessment of the critical nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - understated response to life-threatening situation

jokes.text: Excluding Alaska and Hawaii, the freak states.
jokes.setup: Offering free tickets anywhere in United States
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of Alaska and Hawaii as 'freak states'
jokes.explanation: Good throwaway line that reveals character's mainland bias

jokes.text: It's a chance for you to clean up after us in a whole other state.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Marge a vacation would be good for her
jokes.punchline: His 'sales pitch' is that she can do the same work elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - thinks he's being persuasive while being completely tone-deaf

jokes.text: I wanna explore the world. I wanna watch TV in a different time zone. I wanna visit strange, exotic malls.
jokes.setup: Homer's travel dreams building in epic fashion
jokes.punchline: Mundane goals dressed up as grand adventures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Homer's limited imagination dressed as wanderlust

jokes.text: I'm sick of eating hoagies. I want a grinder, a sub, a footlong hero.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Homer's travel dreams
jokes.punchline: Wanting to experience different names for the same sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - profound desire for trivial regional variations

jokes.text: Oh, great. I specifically asked not to be seated next to a baby.
jokes.setup: Passenger complaining about airline seating
jokes.punchline: Treating baby Maggie like an airline inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Standard airline humor - adequate but not particularly creative

jokes.text: We must be really flying high. Those people down there look all tiny and blurry. Just like the inside of a cataract.
jokes.setup: Grandpa commenting on view from airplane
jokes.punchline: Comparing aerial view to his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good Grandpa humor - turning observation into health complaint

jokes.text: Come on, Bart, they're gonna pamper us. Not literally, of course.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about first class treatment
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that 'pamper' doesn't mean diapers
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that shows Homer's occasional awareness of language

jokes.text: Marge, what's wrong? Are you hungry? Sleepy? Gassy? Gassy? Is it gas? It's gas, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to diagnose Marge's distress
jokes.punchline: His fixation on the gas option
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization - his crude but caring approach to problem-solving

jokes.text: We'll just wait for the killer bees to come to us.
jokes.setup: Homer consoling Marge about not taking the trip
jokes.punchline: His alternative is waiting for a different disaster
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comfort - Homer's twisted optimism about future catastrophes

jokes.text: Sock puppets. Where?! Where?!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming not everyone has fears, Lisa mentions sock puppets
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate terror at the mention
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - Homer's bravado immediately undermined

jokes.text: I just realized we never had a wedding for the cat and the dog. They've been living in sin.
jokes.setup: Marge's anxiety manifesting in strange concerns
jokes.punchline: Worrying about pets' unmarried cohabitation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anxiety manifestation - Marge's moral concerns applied absurdly to pets

jokes.text: Judge, jury and executioner all rolled into one, you are.
jokes.setup: Marge's continuing anxiety spiral
jokes.punchline: Dramatic language about apparently minor domestic issue
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Marge's stress response with dramatic language

jokes.text: Can't talk. Keeping myself in a state of catlike readiness.
jokes.setup: Family trying to talk to Marge
jokes.punchline: Her explanation for hypervigilant behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect anxiety description - 'catlike readiness' perfectly captures hypervigilance

jokes.text: It breaks up families. It turns wives against husbands, children against fathers, neighbors against me.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she doesn't believe in therapy
jokes.punchline: Making it personal at the end with 'neighbors against me'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals Marge's paranoid state through personal insertion

jokes.text: You don't have to pay some fancy psychiatrist 10 bucks an hour to get top-notch therapy.
jokes.setup: Homer offering alternative to expensive therapy
jokes.punchline: Thinking $10/hour is expensive for a psychiatrist
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's disconnect from real costs

jokes.text: You will die a terrible, terrible death. I'm sorry. That was our last caller. Okay, I'm getting something now. You will die a terrible, terrible death.
jokes.setup: Radio psychic reading for callers
jokes.punchline: Same terrible prediction for everyone
jokes.explanation: Good running gag setup showing psychic's one-size-fits-all approach

jokes.text: 'It's Raining Men.'
jokes.setup: Homer calling radio show
jokes.punchline: Requesting the song from earlier that was removed from Moe's jukebox
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that reinforces Homer's musical preference established earlier

jokes.text: Pass me another hunk of copilot.
jokes.setup: Family watching airplane disaster movie
jokes.punchline: Casual cannibalism reference
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's effective but not character-specific

jokes.text: You'll have to live with your grandmother and pick beans.
jokes.setup: Homer warning Lisa about consequences of therapy
jokes.punchline: Specific, oddly mundane apocalyptic scenario
jokes.explanation: Good Homer catastrophizing with specific, weird details

jokes.text: Dad, I like picking beans with Grandma. Well, keep it up, then. Okay, I will. Good. You do that. Fine. You'll be picking many a bean. Hope I do.
jokes.setup: Homer's threat backfires when Lisa likes the activity
jokes.punchline: Extended back-and-forth that becomes increasingly pointless
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how Homer gets trapped in his own logic

jokes.text: I have many, many issues with my beloved smother... Mother.
jokes.setup: Skinner in waiting room explaining his presence
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip revealing his true feelings
jokes.explanation: Classic Freudian slip that perfectly captures Skinner's relationship with his mother

jokes.text: If money is a problem, I charge on a sliding scale. I can go as low as $30 an hour. Keep sliding.
jokes.setup: Therapist offering flexible rates
jokes.punchline: Homer's demand to keep reducing the price
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's shameless approach to negotiation

jokes.text: But it's a few seconds before 12:00.
jokes.setup: Window washer appears exactly at 12:00 as predicted
jokes.punchline: Therapist's precise timing observation
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with precise timing element

jokes.text: They use it to drill a flaming hole in your head.
jokes.setup: Child Marge explaining fire drills
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'fire drill' terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant child logic - literal interpretation of scary-sounding school terminology

jokes.text: And there's only one big toilet, and they make you all go at the same time.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Marge's school fears
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding group bathroom policies
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of child's misinterpretation of school procedures

jokes.text: You know they don't write their own songs. They do so. They don't play their own instruments. No! No! That's not even Michael Nesmith's real hat.
jokes.setup: Kid crushing Marge's love of the Monkees
jokes.punchline: The hat revelation as the final cruel blow
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of childhood cruelty, with fake hat as absurd final detail

jokes.text: The Monkees weren't about music, Marge. They were about rebellion, about political and social upheaval.
jokes.setup: Therapist responding to Monkees discussion
jokes.punchline: Overly serious analysis of manufactured pop group
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of pretentious cultural analysis applied to pop culture

jokes.text: And you also infringed on any number of copyrights.
jokes.setup: Therapist analyzing Marge's dream
jokes.punchline: Casually noting the legal implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging the show's own copyright violations

jokes.text: People always say how small England is, but you couldn't fit it all in here. Not by a long shot.
jokes.setup: Marge deflecting from father discussion
jokes.punchline: Absurd observation about England's size relative to therapy office
jokes.explanation: Perfect anxiety deflection with completely illogical geographical observation

jokes.text: My father was a stewardess.
jokes.setup: Marge finally revealing traumatic memory
jokes.punchline: The stigmatized truth about her father's profession
jokes.explanation: Perfect revelation - explains her flying fear while being unexpectedly progressive for the era

jokes.text: My father was a stewardess.
jokes.setup: Building up to father revelation through flashback
jokes.punchline: The unexpected twist about his actual job
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - we expected pilot trauma, got gender role shame instead. Perfect setup/payoff with great economy.

jokes.text: You might even say he was an American hero. Let's not go nuts.
jokes.setup: Therapist building up the father's pioneering role
jokes.punchline: Marge's deflating response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Marge's modesty even when defending her father

jokes.text: Yes, yes. It's all a rich tapestry.
jokes.setup: Multiple traumatic airplane memories being revealed
jokes.punchline: Therapist's overly literary description of trauma
jokes.explanation: Good satire of therapeutic language making trauma sound artistic

jokes.text: That's okay. You don't have to make her into a superwoman. She can get on the plane, that's plenty.
jokes.setup: Doctor wants to continue therapy
jokes.punchline: Homer's low bar for therapeutic success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer practicality vs. therapeutic idealism

jokes.text: Whenever the wind whistles through the leaves, I'll think, 'Lowenstein. Lowenstein.' My name is Zweig. Lowenstein...
jokes.setup: Marge giving sentimental goodbye
jokes.punchline: Getting therapist's name wrong and ignoring correction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's well-meaning but inattentive nature

jokes.text: That's just the carp swimming around your ankles.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining airplane noises to calm Marge
jokes.punchline: Final 'explanation' is completely impossible
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending - Homer's 'reassurance' becomes completely surreal
